evidence.scientific_clarity: All brief requirements met: 12 study CI lines with inverse-variance weighted squares, red diamond for fixed-effects pooled estimate, dashed null line at 0, effect annotations on right.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype='none' set; matplotlib defaults to DejaVu Sans with no explicit font family override; font sizes (7.5–11pt) are reasonable but the fonttype omission is a hard miss.
evidence.palette: steelblue for individual studies and firebrick for pooled are colorblind-tolerant (distinguishable under deuteranopia/protanopia), but red for the summary diamond is unconventional and could imply directionality.
evidence.layout_density: fig_h=0.45*(n+3) gives ~6.75in for 12 studies; tight_layout used; annotation column adds 0.72 x-units of right margin; divider line separates pooled row cleanly; no obvious overlap.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption precisely matches figure content and includes the pooled estimate with CI; however there is no in-figure legend explaining squares/lines/diamond — reader must rely solely on caption.
evidence.vector_fidelity: figure.pdf is saved but pdf.fonttype is never set to 42, so text is embedded as outlines/paths and will not be selectable or editable in Illustrator/Inkscape.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf, figure.png, figure.svg, caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all exist and are non-trivial; SVG is a bonus beyond the brief's requirements.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption names the effect measure (log OR), describes square sizing (inverse-variance weight), gives the CI formula explicitly, names the pooling method (fixed-effects), and reports the numerical result: 0.245 [0.196, 0.294].

overall_strengths: Scientifically complete forest plot: all brief elements (CI lines, weighted squares, diamond, null line, per-study annotations) are correctly implemented with proper inverse-variance weighting.
Excellent caption with explicit formula, method name, and a specific numerical finding — ready to drop into a manuscript methods/results section.
overall_weaknesses: Missing pdf.fonttype=42 (and svg.fonttype='none') means text in the PDF/SVG is not selectable or editable — a standard journal submission requirement that will require a revision.

No in-figure legend and default DejaVu Sans font; adding a brief legend (square=study, diamond=pooled) and switching to a serif or sans-serif matching the manuscript (e.g. Helvetica/Arial) would improve reviewer readiness.
